title: AGENTOBS EvalPayload
description: RFC-0001 §7.2 — Payload for evaluation quality tracking events in the llm.eval.* namespace. Covers four event types: llm.eval.score.recorded (quality score attached to a span or run), llm.eval.regression.detected (quality regression flagged), llm.eval.scenario.started (eval scenario lifecycle), llm.eval.scenario.completed. Continuous eval on production traffic enables quality regression detection without a dedicated test suite run.

EvalScoreRecordedPayload.title: EvalScoreRecordedPayload
EvalScoreRecordedPayload.description: Payload for llm.eval.score.recorded — a quality score produced by an evaluator and attached to a span or agent run.

evaluator.description: Identifier of the system or model that produced this score (e.g. 'gpt-4o-judge', 'human-reviewer', 'rule-based:fluency').

metric_name.description: Name of the evaluation metric. SHOULD use a consistent naming convention across the deployment (e.g. 'coherence', 'faithfulness', 'answer_relevance', 'toxicity').

score.description: Numeric score value. Scale and range are metric-specific; document the scale via metric_scale.

score_min.description: Minimum possible value for this metric's scale.

score_max.description: Maximum possible value for this metric's scale.

threshold.description: Passing threshold for this metric. Scores below threshold indicate quality concern.

passed.description: Whether the score meets or exceeds the threshold. If threshold is absent, this field SHOULD be omitted.

subject_event_id.description: ULID of the event (span, step, or run) this score is evaluating.

eval_run_id.description: Identifier grouping multiple eval scores from the same evaluation batch or pipeline run.

rationale.description: Human or LLM-generated explanation for the score. SHOULD be included for LLM-judge evaluators to support auditability.
examples: The response correctly answers the question with supporting evidence and no hallucination detected.

model.description: Model used as the judge, when evaluator is an LLM.

EvalRegressionDetectedPayload.title: EvalRegressionDetectedPayload
EvalRegressionDetectedPayload.description: Payload for llm.eval.regression.detected — a statistically significant quality regression has been detected relative to a baseline.

metric_name.description: Evaluation metric on which regression was detected.

baseline_score.description: Historical baseline score used as the comparison reference.

current_score.description: Current score that triggered the regression alert.

delta.description: Absolute change: current_score - baseline_score. Negative values indicate degradation.

regression_pct.description: Percentage change relative to baseline: (delta / baseline_score) * 100.

eval_run_id.description: Evaluation pipeline run that detected the regression.

sample_count.description: Number of samples evaluated to compute current_score.

EvalScenarioStartedPayload.title: EvalScenarioStartedPayload
EvalScenarioStartedPayload.description: Payload for llm.eval.scenario.started — lifecycle event marking the beginning of a named evaluation scenario.

scenario_id.description: Unique identifier for this evaluation scenario.

scenario_name.description: Human-readable name for the evaluation scenario.

dataset_id.description: Identifier of the evaluation dataset used.

expected_sample_count.description: Number of samples expected to be evaluated in this scenario.

metrics.description: List of metric names that will be evaluated in this scenario.

EvalScenarioCompletedPayload.title: EvalScenarioCompletedPayload
EvalScenarioCompletedPayload.description: Payload for llm.eval.scenario.completed — lifecycle event marking the end of a named evaluation scenario.

scenario_id.description: Unique identifier matching the scenario_id from the corresponding llm.eval.scenario.started event.

duration_ms.description: Total time taken to run the complete scenario in milliseconds.

aggregate_scores.description: Map of metric_name → mean score across all evaluated samples.

regressions_detected.description: Count of metrics that triggered regression alerts during this scenario.
